title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will cut budget 40% in major reset
summary: The Ethereum Foundation is reducing its budget by 40% and cutting staff by 20%, following the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang. This marks the ninth senior departure since January, signaling significant changes within the organization.
body:
Vitalik Buterin announced that the Ethereum Foundation (EF) will cut its budget by 40% as part of a major organizational reset. The reduction comes on the same day the EF confirmed a 20% reduction in headcount and follows the resignation of co-Executive Director Hsiao-Wei Wang. Her departure brings the total number of senior Ethereum Foundation figures to leave since January to nine, underscoring the scale of the organization's ongoing turmoil.

The budget reduction and staff cuts come as the EF faces internal challenges and seeks to optimize its resources. The departure of key leaders, including Wang, highlights ongoing turbulence within the organization. Since January, nine senior Ethereum Foundation figures have resigned, underscoring the scale of the changes taking place.

These changes could impact the development and support of Ethereum-related projects. The budget cuts may affect funding for research, development, and community initiatives, potentially slowing down some aspects of Ethereum's ecosystem. However, the foundation assures that core development efforts will remain a priority.

For crypto enthusiasts and investors, this news signals a period of transition for the Ethereum Foundation. While the budget cuts and staff reductions may raise concerns, they also indicate a strategic shift towards more efficient operations. Watch for further announcements from the EF regarding how these changes will impact specific projects and initiatives.
